title: cephalocarpus rigidus var. typicus
intro: Cephalocarpus rigidus var. typicus, also known as the "Black Snake Root" or "Rattlesnake Root," is a perennial plant species belonging to the Saxifragaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Cephalocarpus rigidus var. typicus include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate rheumatic pains. Cephalocarpus rigidus var. typicus is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and its roots are often dried and used in herbal teas.
